rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 21, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT KARI RILEY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Deposition testimony of co-defendant Jaclyn Fenton establishes a genuine dispute of material fact exists as to who was managing the property at the time Plaintiff's stored objects were discarded. Defendant's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. California law allows for recovery for negligent infliction of emotional distress without a physical injury where the parties had a pre-existing duty of care, such as a tenant-landlord relationship (See Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 10 Cal.App.3d 903, 918.) = (501/REQ)
